varieties.name: Queensland Blue
varieties.traits: Medium to large fruit (2-5kg), dark grey-blue ribbed skin, thin skin, classic Australian variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, deep orange, dry, stringless, sweet flavor
varieties.growing_notes: Ideal for subtropical climate. Can be grown year-round in humid subtropical climates and tropical regions. Excellent humidity tolerance. Handles warm, moist conditions exceptionally well. One of Australia's most iconic pumpkins.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Diggers Club, Succeed Heirlooms, widely available throughout Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Jarrahdale
varieties.origin: Jarrahdale, Western Australia (cross between Blue Hubbard and Cinderella, introduced to US 1932)
varieties.traits: Medium-large (5-8kg), blue-grey-green skin with prominent ribbing, flattened shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden-orange, thick, sweet, mildly flavored, firm and non-stringy

varieties.name: Kent (Jap Pumpkin)
varieties.traits: Medium (4-5kg), dark green skin mottled with yellow-brown patches, round shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light orange, firm, excellent nutty flavor, sweet

varieties.name: Butternut Waltham
varieties.origin: Waltham, Massachusetts, USA (developed 1940s by Charles Leggett)
varieties.traits: Small to medium (1.5-2.5kg), pear-shaped, pale tan/beige skin, elongated neck
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, smooth texture, sweet and nutty (smooth as butter, sweet as a nut)
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, 6kg per plant, 4-5 fruits per vine
varieties.growing_notes: Grows well in Humid subtropical subtropical climate. Small seed cavity. Intermediate powdery mildew resistance. Stores extremely well (up to 6 months). One of the world's most popular culinary pumpkins.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Diggers Club, Eden Seeds, widely available

varieties.name: Ironbark
varieties.traits: Large, dark green skin, thick tough slightly bumpy skin

varieties.name: Australian Butter
varieties.traits: Large (up to 6kg), pale orange-yellow skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, dry, orange, small seed cavity
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for baking and roasting. Outstanding storage qualities. Well-suited to Humid subtropical climate. Rare Australian heirloom.
varieties.availability: Diggers Club (members), specialty Australian seed savers

varieties.name: Triamble
varieties.traits: Large (3-5kg), unique three-lobed shape, thick blue-grey skin, distinctive shamrock appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow to deep orange, dry, sweet, rich tasting

varieties.name: Golden Nugget
varieties.traits: Small (10-15cm diameter), bright orange skin, flattened spherical shape, compact bush variety
varieties.growing_notes: Can be grown year-round in tropical and subtropical Australia including humid subtropical climates. Compact bush makes it ideal for small spaces. Good storage (2+ months). Susceptible to powdery mildew in humid conditions.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Succeed Heirlooms, widely available

varieties.name: Buttercup
varieties.traits: Small to medium (2kg), dark green skin with grey stripes, flattish round shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden yellow, dense, fibreless, nutty flavor

varieties.name: Blue Hubbard
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, warty blue-grey skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow-orange, sweet, rich, nutty flavor, dry texture

varieties.name: Anna Swartz
varieties.origin: Family heirloom passed to Anna Swartz in 1950s
varieties.traits: Small to medium (2-4kg), dark blue-green mottled skin, extremely hard shell
varieties.flesh_fruit: High-quality yellow-orange (similar to sweet potato), sweet flavor

varieties.name: Black Futsu
varieties.traits: Average 2kg, heavily fluted skin, black when picked, transforms to dusty orange in storage
varieties.flesh_fruit: Exceptionally sweet, nutty, flavor improves in storage

varieties.name: Musquee de Provence
varieties.origin: Southern France (most popular in Paris markets 1880s)
varieties.traits: Large (9-10kg), rich brown color when ripe, heavily ribbed, smooth skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, moderately sweet, thick, very fine flavored
varieties.growing_notes: C. moschata species handles Humid subtropical heat and humidity well. Excellent storage potential. Well-suited to baking and pies. Highly ornamental.
varieties.availability: Boondie Seeds, The Lost Seed, specialty French variety suppliers

varieties.name: Jack O'Lantern
varieties.traits: Medium-large (3-4.5kg), round to oblong, bright orange
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, makes good pies and soups despite carving designation
varieties.growing_notes: Vines grow long and need room. Can handle Humid subtropical warm conditions. Good for decoration and cooking.
varieties.availability: Common in Australian nurseries and seed suppliers

varieties.name: Sweet Dumpling
varieties.traits: Small (250-340g, 10cm diameter), squat compact shape, cream and green striped skin
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect single-serve size. Ideal for stuffing and baking. No curing required. Can be used directly from vine. Grows well in humid subtropical climates.
varieties.availability: Southern Harvest, specialty seed suppliers

varieties.name: Turk's Turban
varieties.traits: Small, decorative, resembles turban shape, colorful striped skin
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent roasted. Stores well. Highly ornamental. Grows in Humid subtropical climate.
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms, Boondie Seeds, Diggers Club

varieties.name: Orange Hokkaido (Red Kuri)
varieties.origin: Japan (also known as Uchiki Kuri or Baby Red Hubbard)
varieties.traits: Small-medium, brilliant red-orange, teardrop shaped
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, smooth, nutty, sweet, chestnut-like flavor

varieties.name: Galeux d'Eysines
varieties.traits: Medium-large, distinctive peanut shell appearance with warted surface, salmon-pink skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, smooth, sweet, excellent quality
varieties.growing_notes: Unique appearance. Grows in humid subtropical climates conditions. Excellent keeper. Highly ornamental and delicious.
varieties.availability: Boondie Seeds, Diggers Club, specialty French variety sources

varieties.name: Atlantic Giant
varieties.origin: Windsor, Nova Scotia (developed by Howard Dill, 1978)
varieties.traits: Giant size (200+ pounds common, can reach 1000+ pounds), orange, rough skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Not ideal for eating, grown primarily for size
varieties.yield_notes: Usually 1 pumpkin per plant when grown for size
varieties.growing_notes: Struggles in tropical climates but can be attempted in humid subtropical climates with care. Best in cooler regions. Requires extensive space. Descended from French Jaune Gros de Paris.
varieties.availability: Diggers Club, specialty competition pumpkin suppliers

varieties.name: Silver Edge (Cushaw)
varieties.traits: Round or pear-shaped, white with mottled green stripes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good eating, but primarily grown for large tasty silver-edged seeds

varieties.name: Kumi Kumi (Kamo Kamo)
varieties.traits: Heavily ribbed, speckled green, soft skin, 20-30cm across

varieties.name: Small Sugar (New England Pie)
varieties.traits: Small (2-3kg, 20cm across), deep orange, round
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, fine-grained, deep orange, excellent for pies
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional pie pumpkin. Grows in humid subtropical climates. Stores well. Classic American heirloom.
varieties.availability: The Lost Seed, Happy Valley Seeds, Southern Harvest

varieties.name: Big Max
varieties.traits: Large, round, bright orange, can grow massive under ideal conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, fine-grained, mild-flavored, less suitable for roasting
varieties.growing_notes: Great for pies and desserts. Can be carved. Stores well. Suitable for humid subtropical climates growing.
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms, Happy Valley Seeds, common availability

varieties.name: Ambili 🧬
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), Vellanikkara, tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, flat-round fruits (4-6kg), moderately incised leaves
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding, comparable to CO-2 and Suvarna, average 1.47 fruits per plant
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically developed for tropical monsoon climates conditions. Local favorite for curries and stews. Excellent for traditional mathanga erissery. Well-adapted to monsoon climate and high humidity. Dark green leaf blade.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, tropical monsoon climates state agricultural stores, local tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Arka Suryamukhi
varieties.origin: Indian Institute of Horticultural Research (IIHR), Bangalore
varieties.traits: Round with pressed ends (typically 1kg when mature), orange-red with white stripes, flattish round shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, firm, bright orange, high carotene content (3331 IU/100g), deep orange color
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yield - up to 34 tons per hectare

varieties.name: Arka Chandan
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, early-maturing hybrid, golden-orange skin, pleasant aroma
varieties.growing_notes: Highly resistant to common pumpkin diseases. Excellent for Tropical monsoon monsoon conditions. Strong aromatic flesh prized in traditional sweets and desserts (petha, halwa). Well-suited to both moderate and dry zones.
varieties.availability: IIHR, tropical monsoon climates agricultural stores, seed companies across India

varieties.name: Pusa Vishwas (Pusa Viswas)
varieties.traits: Long shelf life variety, cream-brown colored fruit skin at maturity
varieties.yield_notes: Very high (31.9-40.4 t/ha across different locations)
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically suitable for tropical and subtropical growing. Excellent for North Indian plains but also performs well in tropical monsoon climates. Wide adaptability to various Indian agro-climatic zones. Used for both culinary and decorative purposes.
varieties.availability: IARI Delhi, widely available through Indian agricultural universities, tropical monsoon climates agricultural stores

varieties.name: CO-1 🧬
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University (TNAU), Tamil Nadu
varieties.traits: Round shape, cream-brown fruit skin at maturity, creamy texture
varieties.flesh_fruit: Orange color, sweet and flavorful pulp

varieties.name: CO-2 🧬
varieties.traits: Average fruit weight 1.5-2kg, moderately incised leaves, dark green leaf blade

varieties.name: Suvarna 🧬
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), Vellanikkara
varieties.traits: High fruit dimensions, cream-brown skin at maturity, dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: High values for seed traits, vigorous variety
varieties.growing_notes: Developed specifically for tropical monsoon climates conditions. Excellent monsoon tolerance. Large fruits. Vigorous growth. Popular in local tropical monsoon climates markets.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, tropical monsoon climates state agricultural stores

varieties.name: Saras 🧬
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), Vellanikkara
varieties.traits: Yellowish-brown skin at maturity, intermediate vigor habit

varieties.name: Sooraj 🧬
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University (KAU), Vellanikkara
varieties.traits: Developed for tropical monsoon climates conditions

varieties.name: Punjab Samrat
varieties.traits: Small round fruits, medium long vines, angular stem, dark green leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden yellow, mottled green when immature, pale brown at maturity
varieties.growing_notes: Resistant to powdery mildew - crucial for Tropical monsoon humid climate. While developed for North India, grows well in tropical conditions. Disease resistance makes it suitable for monsoon regions.
varieties.availability: Punjab Agricultural University, available through seed suppliers in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Pusa Hybrid-1
varieties.traits: Spherical medium-sized fruits (5kg average), dark green leaves with white spots
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, suitable for commercial cultivation
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for both summer and kharif (monsoon) seasons. Vigorous growth. Widely grown across different agro-climatic zones including tropical monsoon climates. High commercial value.
varieties.availability: IARI Delhi, widely available in tropical monsoon climates through agricultural stores and seed companies

varieties.name: Pusa Vikas
varieties.traits: High-yielding variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, suitable for culinary use
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for Indian conditions. Grows well in Tropical monsoon tropical climate. Popular among commercial growers.
varieties.availability: IARI, available through seed distributors in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Narendra Agrim
varieties.origin: Narendra Deva University of Agriculture and Technology (NDUAT)
varieties.traits: Early-maturing variety, medium-sized fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Ideal for commercial cultivation. Early maturity beneficial for multiple cropping. Can be grown in Tropical monsoon year-round warm climate.
varieties.availability: NDUAT, available through agricultural universities and tropical monsoon climates seed suppliers

varieties.name: Narendra Amrit
varieties.origin: Narendra Deva University of Agriculture and Technology
varieties.traits: Improved variety for commercial production
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited for tropical growing. Can adapt to Tropical monsoon monsoon conditions with proper management.
varieties.availability: NDUAT, available through seed companies serving tropical monsoon climates market

varieties.name: Kashi Harit
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, early-maturing
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for Indian conditions. Can be grown in tropical monsoon climates with good results. Early maturity beneficial for monsoon cropping.
varieties.availability: ICAR-IIVR, agricultural universities, tropical monsoon climates seed suppliers

varieties.name: VRM 5-10
varieties.traits: Disease-resistant variety
varieties.growing_notes: Resistant to green mottle virus and downy mildew - extremely important for Tropical monsoon humid monsoon climate. Disease resistance makes it valuable for tropical cultivation.
varieties.availability: Available through agricultural universities and tropical monsoon climates seed distributors

varieties.name: CM 14 (Solan Badami)
varieties.origin: YS Parmar University of Horticulture and Forestry, Solan, Himachal Pradesh
varieties.traits: Nutty, sweet flavor, fine texture
varieties.flesh_fruit: High quality, excellent for culinary use
varieties.growing_notes: While developed for hill regions, performs well in various climates. C. moschata species makes it suitable for tropical conditions including tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: YSP University Solan, available through national seed networks to tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Butternut (General)
varieties.traits: Pear-shaped, tan/beige skin, elongated

varieties.name: Azad Kaddu
varieties.traits: Medium-sized variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, suitable for Indian cuisine
varieties.growing_notes: Can be grown in Tropical monsoon tropical climate. Suitable for commercial cultivation.
varieties.availability: Available through agricultural stores in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: CS 14
varieties.traits: Commercial variety
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Tropical monsoon tropical climate. Popular among commercial growers.
varieties.availability: Agricultural stores in tropical monsoon climates and South India

varieties.name: IIHR 93-1-1-1
varieties.traits: Experimental/breeding line variety
varieties.growing_notes: Developed by IIHR for Indian tropical conditions. Suitable for tropical monsoon climates growing.
varieties.availability: IIHR Bangalore, research institutions, limited commercial availability

varieties.name: Kalyanpur
varieties.traits: Regional variety

varieties.name: NDPK 24
varieties.traits: Breeding line variety
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for Indian conditions. Can be grown in tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: NDUAT, research networks, limited availability

varieties.name: CM-350
varieties.traits: Commercial variety
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for tropical growing. Can be cultivated in tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: Agricultural stores in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: PAU Magaz Kaddoo-1
varieties.traits: Hull-less seeds, dwarf vines, dark green leaves, medium-sized round fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Used for making magaz (seeds) and snacks. Hull-less seeds are specialty feature. Can be grown in tropical monsoon climates for seed production.
varieties.availability: Punjab Agricultural University, available through seed networks to tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Thai Rai Kaw Tok 🏆
varieties.traits: Adapted to tropical conditions

varieties.name: Kent (for Kerala)
varieties.traits: Medium (4-5kg), green mottled skin
varieties.growing_notes: Being C. moschata, can handle Tropical monsoon tropical climate. Long season suits year-round growing. Tolerates heat and humidity. Popular in tropical Asian countries.
varieties.availability: May be available through tropical monsoon climates import networks from Asian seed suppliers
